canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, the dominant framing of Perplexity is as a quantitative metric for evaluating language model outputs, though a distinct minority of papers use the name to refer to a commercial LLM search engine. In its most common usage as a measurement, it is operationalized to assess various aspects of text quality, such as fluency, coherence, and naturalness, with one paper describing it as a "lightweight proxy for naturalness" ("DICE: Structured Reasoning inLLMs throughSLM-Guided Chain-of-Thought Correction"). This general application is reflected in its use to measure constructs like Utility preservation, Faithfulness, and Imperceptibility. The evaluation procedure is sometimes specified as using a larger language model to calculate the perplexity of text generated by a smaller one. Beyond general quality assessment, Perplexity is also adapted into more specialized methods, including a "Perplexity Score" baseline for detecting pre-training data exposure and "sequence perplexity" as a value probing technique. In contrast, a smaller set of work treats "Perplexity" as a proper noun, identifying it as one of the "production LLM search engines" used as a target for evaluation.
expert_removed_reason: metric/method/tool — not a benchmark or LLM ability

Revealing Weaknesses in Text Watermarking Through Self-Information Rewrite Attacks

definitions.text: A production LLM search engine used as a black-box evaluation target for recommendation and citation manipulation.
definitions.paper: Adversarial Search Engine Optimization for Large Language Models

definitions.text: A baseline method for detecting pre-training data exposure by using the model's perplexity on a given sample as an indicator of whether it was seen during training.
definitions.paper: How Contaminated Is Your Benchmark? Measuring Dataset Leakage in Large Language Models with Kernel Divergence

definitions.text: A value probing method that calculates a normalized probability distribution over options based on the inverse perplexity of the complete answer sequence, including both the option token and its text.
definitions.paper: QFrCoLA: aQuebec-French Corpus of Linguistic Acceptability Judgments

definitions.text: A language-model quality evaluation procedure reporting perplexity for quantized OPT and LLaMA models under different quantization settings.
definitions.paper: LUT-GEMM: Quantized Matrix Multiplication based on LUTs for Efficient Inference in Large-Scale Generative Language Models
